skill.name: i18n-localization
skill.description: Internationalization and localization patterns. Detecting hardcoded strings, managing translations, locale files, RTL support.
skill.summary: Detects hardcoded strings and manages translations for multi-language applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill provides i18n best practices documentation and a Python checker script. No actual security risks detected.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers detect hardcoded strings and manage translations for multilingual applications. It provides patterns for React, Vue, and Python projects.
actual_capabilities: Detect hardcoded strings in React, Vue, and Python files
Check locale file completeness across languages
Provide i18n implementation patterns for React and Next.js
Guide on RTL language support for Arabic and Hebrew
Offer gettext patterns for Python applications
limitations: Does not perform automatic translation of strings
Does not modify code to add i18n functions
Cannot detect strings in image assets or videos
Requires manual implementation of recommended patterns

use_cases.title: Audit existing codebase for i18n readiness
use_cases.description: Run the i18n checker to find hardcoded strings and missing translation keys in your codebase
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on multilingual web applications

use_cases.title: Learn i18n best practices
use_cases.description: Reference the documentation to implement proper internationalization patterns for your framework

use_cases.title: Set up RTL language support
use_cases.description: Configure your application for right-to-left languages using CSS logical properties

prompt_templates.title: Check for hardcoded strings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the i18n checker script to scan my project directory for hardcoded strings. Run: python scripts/i18n_checker.py <project_path>. Analyze the results and explain what needs to be translated.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to audit your codebase for untranslated strings

prompt_templates.title: Implement React i18n
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up react-i18next in a React project. Include the provider setup, translation file structure, and how to use the useTranslation hook in components.
prompt_templates.scenario: When adding internationalization to a React application

prompt_templates.title: Add RTL support
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to add RTL (right-to-left) support for Arabic or Hebrew. Show the CSS changes needed and how to handle direction switching.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing your app for Arabic or Hebrew users

prompt_templates.title: Check locale file consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the i18n checker to verify that all translation keys exist across my locale JSON files. Report any missing keys per language.

output_examples.input: Run i18n checker on my React project
output: The checker scans your code files and locale JSONs, then reports found languages, missing translation keys, and files with hardcoded strings that need conversion to use translation keys.

output_examples.input: How do I set up i18n in Next.js?
output: Use next-intl library. Create a messages folder with locale JSON files, set up the provider in your layout, and use the useTranslations hook to access translations in components.

output_examples.input: What are the best practices for i18n?
output: Use translation keys instead of raw text, namespace translations by feature, support pluralization with ICU format, use Intl API for dates and numbers, and plan for RTL from the start.

best_practices: Use translation keys (like 'common.buttons.submit') instead of raw text strings
Organize translations by feature namespace (auth.json, errors.json, etc.)
Use CSS logical properties (margin-inline-start) for RTL compatibility
anti_patterns: Hardcoding user-facing strings directly in components
Concatenating translated strings with regular text
Assuming text length stays the same across languages

faq.question: What is the difference between i18n and L10n?
faq.answer: i18n (internationalization) is the process of making your app translatable. L10n (localization) is the actual translation work for a specific locale.

faq.question: Does this skill translate strings automatically?
faq.answer: No, this skill detects hardcoded strings and guides you on patterns. Translation must be done manually or with separate translation services.

faq.question: Which frameworks does the checker support?
faq.answer: The i18n_checker.py script supports React, Vue, and Python projects.

faq.question: How do I handle RTL languages like Arabic?
faq.answer: Use CSS logical properties instead of left/right, add dir='rtl' attribute to html, and test with languages like Arabic or Hebrew.

faq.question: What are translation namespaces?
faq.answer: Namespaces organize translations into groups like common, auth, errors. This keeps translation files manageable for large applications.

faq.question: How do I handle pluralization?
faq.answer: Use ICU message format which supports plural rules like {count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}
